metadata.title: MedVault Terraform/AWS Component Definition
metadata.remarks: Component Definition for the Terraform-managed AWS infrastructure layer of MedVault. Describes which FedRAMP Moderate controls this component implements and how.

components.title: MedVault Terraform/AWS Infrastructure
components.description: Terraform 1.7+ modules deploying AWS resources (S3, KMS, IAM) to host MedVault. Pre-apply policy enforcement via Checkov in CI.
components.purpose: Provide the FedRAMP Moderate-aligned cloud infrastructure substrate for MedVault.

control-implementations.description: Controls satisfied by the Terraform/AWS component, per the MedVault tailored FedRAMP Moderate profile.

implemented-requirements.description: Least privilege is enforced at the IAM layer. The terraform-medvault IAM user has s3:* and a curated KMS action set, both scoped exclusively to medvault-* resource prefixes. The user cannot enumerate other buckets in the account; this was verified by an intentional AccessDenied on s3:ListAllMyBuckets during build. Policy document: docs/iam-policy.json.

S3 server access logging is enabled on the medvault-reports-dev bucket, with logs delivered to a separate medvault-access-logs-dev bucket. The separation ensures audit trail integrity even if the primary bucket is compromised. Implemented via aws_s3_bucket_logging resource in infrastructure/s3-bucket.tf.

Configuration baseline is enforced by Checkov scanning of all Terraform code on every push. The CI workflow (.github/workflows/terraform-scan.yml) blocks merges when Checkov finds violations. Specific configurations enforced: KMS encryption on data buckets (CKV_AWS_19, CKV_AWS_145), public access blocked four ways (CKV_AWS_53-56), TLS-only access policies, versioning, separate logging bucket.

Cryptographic protection of data at rest is implemented via AWS KMS customer-managed keys with annual rotation enabled (enable_key_rotation = true). The aws_kms_key.reports resource encrypts the primary data bucket using SSE-KMS with bucket key enabled. Verified via aws s3api get-bucket-encryption returning the customer-managed KMS key ARN. The access logs bucket uses AES256 (S3-managed) intentionally to avoid circular KMS dependency; AES256 is FIPS 140-2 validated. POA&M item POAM-001 tracks the addition of an explicit KMS key policy.

Protection of information at rest is achieved via the same KMS-SSE configuration described in SC-13. All MedVault S3 buckets (reports and access logs) are encrypted by default; no unencrypted data can be stored. Bucket key enabled reduces KMS API calls and cost without weakening protection.

Transmission confidentiality is enforced by the bucket policy aws_s3_bucket_policy.reports_tls_only, which denies any s3:* action when aws:SecureTransport is false. This forces all access to use TLS, regardless of caller. Combined with AWS's default TLS 1.2+ on S3 endpoints, this provides SC-8 compliance.

resources.title: MedVault Tailored FedRAMP Moderate Profile
resources.description: The profile this Component Definition references for its implementations.
